The entrapment efficiencies of the HARF and AA are presented in Table 2. 
The high entrapment efficiencies of HARF and AA are attributed to the PEG coating 
the PLGA NPs founding a barrier that surrounds and protects the loaded drugs, prevent-
ing their loss during the homogenization step and retaining their stability [52]. The capac-
ity to entrap high concentrations of drugs supports sustained drug release [53].
